title: [v4.7] [CI:DOCS]rtd: implement v2 build file
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #20340

/assign baude

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *openshift-cherrypick-robot* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.7/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

Failure is persistent:
```
Check Yaml...............................................................Failed
- hook id: check-yaml
- exit code: 1
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/.cache/pre-commit/repom78gpk4y/py_env-python3/bin/check-yaml", line 5, in <module>
    from pre_commit_hooks.check_yaml import main
  File "/root/.cache/pre-commit/repom78gpk4y/py_env-python3/lib/python3.11/site-packages/pre_commit_hooks/check_yaml.py", line 10, in <module>
    yaml = ruamel.yaml.YAML(typ='safe')
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: module 'ruamel.yaml' has no attribute 'YAML'
```
...and only on aarch64 (the other Validates pass). It's tempting to think "this is a YAML test failure, and this PR adds a YAML file", but that doesn't make sense. I'm wondering if the yaml-check test is broken on this arch.
i think we can ignore the failure?
